generated_files.probe: The minimized original-path buildCookieHeader driver verifies generated PHP syntax, public by-reference ABI, behavior parity, and manifest core_ir_features for native-array IR nodes.

claims: WPHX PHP now has reusable PHP-core statement/expression IR nodes and a printer for if statements, foreach loops, native array reads and writes, array casts, long array literals, object construction, local variables, assignments, function calls, method calls, and static calls.

The WP_Http::buildCookieHeader profile adapter now selects the WordPress public boundary shape but emits its native-array body through those reusable core IR nodes, not through a durable WordPress-only PHP body template.
WPHX-COMP-PHP.07 processHeaders can depend on these native-array core IR nodes as its next compiler-pressure substrate.
non_claims: This does not claim arbitrary Haxe typed-expression lowering into PHP-core IR.
This does not claim a full custom PHP backend or extracted reflaxe.php target.
This does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership, WP_Http::processHeaders generation, or installed WordPress distribution behavior.
